title: Velvety Turkish Bean Soup
description: A rich, smooth bean soup with a hint of citrus and ginger, perfect for easing hangovers and satisfying pescatarian cravings.
instructions: Soak the dried beans overnight, rinse and drain them before cooking.
Bring a pot of water to a boil, blanch the beans for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a separate saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add the grated ginger, ground cumin, ground turmeric, and tomato paste; cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in the blanched beans and fish stock, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes until the beans are tender and the broth is thickened.
Add the shrimp and simmer for an additional 3-4 minutes until the shrimp turn pink and are cooked through.
Stir in the lemon juice and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Using an immersion blender, blend the soup in the pot until silky and velvety. If preferred, transfer half of the soup to a countertop blender and blend before returning it to the pot.
Serve the soup hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
